{{Short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Niger women's national football team}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Niger National Football Team 
| Nickname           = Ménas
| Badge              = 
| Association        = [[Nigerien Football Federation|Fédération Nigerienne de Football]] (FENIFOOT)
| Confederation      = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Sub-confederation  = [[West African Football Union|WAFU]] (West Africa)
| Coach              = [[Ezzaki Badou]] 
| Captain            = [[Abdoulaye Boureima Katkoré|Abdoulaye Katkoré]]
| Most caps          = [[Kassaly Daouda]] (89)
| Top scorer         = [[Victorien Adebayor]] (22)
| Home Stadium       = [[Stade Général Seyni Kountché]]
| FIFA Trigramme     = NIG
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|NIG}}
| FIFA max           = 68
| FIFA max date      = November 1994
| FIFA min           = 196
| FIFA min date      = August 2002
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Niger}}
| Elo max            = 103
| Elo max date       = July 1991
| Elo min            = 146
| Elo min date       = December 1980
| pattern_la1        = 
| pattern_b1         = _nig2526a
| pattern_ra1        = 
| pattern_sh1        =_nig2526a 
| leftarm1           = FF5500
| body1              = FF5500
| rightarm1          = FF5500
| shorts1            = FF5500
| socks1             = FF5500
| pattern_la2        = 
| pattern_b2         = _nig2526h
| pattern_ra2        = 
| pattern_sh2        =_nig2526h
| leftarm2           = ffffff
| body2              = ffffff
| rightarm2          = ffffff
| shorts2            = ffffff
| socks2             = ffffff
| First game         = {{fb|CHA}} 2–2 {{fb-rt|NIG}} <br> ([[Abidjan]], [[Ivory Coast]]; 25 December 1961)
| Largest win        = {{fb|NIG}} 7–1 {{fb-rt|MTN|1959}} <br> ([[Niamey]], [[Niger]]; 12 October 1990)<br/>{{fb|NIG}} 6–0 {{fb-rt|BOE}}<br>([[Casablanca]], [[Morocco]]; 25 March 2025)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|CGO}} 10–0 {{fb-rt|NIG}} <br> ([[Abidjan]], [[Ivory Coast]]; 27 December 1961)
| Regional name      = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps  = 2
| Regional cup first = [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]
| Regional cup best  = Group stage ([[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]], [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]])
|2ndRegional cup apps=4|2ndRegional name=[[African Nations Championship]]|2ndRegional cup first=[[2011 African Nations Championship|2011]]|2ndRegional cup best=Fourth place ([[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]])|pattern_so2=_orangetop}}

The '''Niger national football team''' ([[French language|French]]: '' Équipe de football du Niger'')<ref>[http://www.starafrica.com/en/football/news/article/view/orange-2012-afcon-qualifiers-130-millio-167435.html Orange 2012 Afcon qualifiers :130 Million FCFA for the Menas] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111109012021/http://www.starafrica.com/en/football/news/article/view/orange-2012-afcon-qualifiers-130-millio-167435.html |date=2011-11-09}}. 22/05/2011 StarAfrica sports.</ref><ref>[http://www.cafonline.com/competition/african-cup-of-nations_2012/news/6795-menas-to-test-pharaohs-form.html Menas to test Pharaohs form] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101013164417/http://www.cafonline.com/competition/african-cup-of-nations_2012/news/6795-menas-to-test-pharaohs-form.html |date=2010-10-13}}. Confederation of African Football. 10-09-2010</ref> represents [[Niger]] in [[FIFA|international]] [[association football|football]] through the [[Nigerien Football Federation]], a member of [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF). Niger plays in the colors of the [[flag of Niger]], white, green, orange and purple. Their nickname comes from the [[Dama gazelle]], native to Niger, the [[Hausa language|Hausa]] name of which is ''Meyna'' or ''Ménas''<ref>[http://www.saharaconservation.org/?Dama-Gazelle Dama Gazelle Nanger dama] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924093523/http://www.saharaconservation.org/?Dama-Gazelle |date=2015-09-24}}. Sahara Conservation Fund, 2007, 2011.</ref> The Dama appears on their badge in the colors of the national flag.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.afrik-foot.com/can-2019-la-liste-du-niger-contre-l-egypte-sans-maazou|title=CAN 2019 : la liste du Niger contre l'Egypte sans Maazou|date=March 21, 2019|website=Afrik-Foot|access-date=November 15, 2021|archive-date=November 15, 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211115201339/https://www.afrik-foot.com/can-2019-la-liste-du-niger-contre-l-egypte-sans-maazou|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/134/2019/Niger.html|title=Niger team of 2019|website=national-football-teams.com|publisher=National Football Teams|access-date=2019-03-30|archive-date=2019-03-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190330175508/https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/134/2019/Niger.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

==History==
Although one of the less successful sides in the strong West Africa region, Niger has produced a couple of noteworthy runs in qualifying tournaments.

One of their best performances was in the [[1982 FIFA World Cup]] qualifiers in which Niger eliminated [[Somalia national football team|Somalia]] and [[Togo national football team|Togo]] on the [[away goals rule]], but were beaten by [[Algeria national football team|Algeria]] in the third round where only eight teams were left. Notable players in this run included Jacques Komlan, Hassane Adamou and [[Moussa Kanfideni]].

In 1990, they set a record by thrashing [[Mauritania national football team|Mauritania]] 7–1 in continental qualifiers, the highest positive score margin for the ''Mena''.

In the [[2004 African Nations Cup]] qualifiers, Niger won all their home games (including a win over [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]]) to finish on nine points, just three short of qualification.

The Niger squad is also plagued by financial concerns, which have caused them to withdraw from international tournaments on more than one occasion. The [[Nigerien Football Federation]] would have turned to fundraising to pay for their trip to the [[2010 African Cup of Nations]] in [[Angola]], had they qualified.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.helpmena.com/index.html |title=Project MENA is dedicated to assisting MENA, the Niger national soccer team. The project aimed to raise enough money to send the team to compete for the 2010 African Cup in Angola. |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080426002616/http://www.helpmena.com/index.html |archive-date=2008-04-26}}</ref>

On 10 October 2010, Niger earned a shock 1–0 win over [[Egypt national football team|Egypt]] at home in the [[2012 African Cup of Nations qualification]].

Despite a failed run for AFCON 2010, Niger hosted and won the [[UEMOA Tournament]] in November 2010, and followed up with their first ever qualification for the African Nations Championship in February 2011.<ref>[http://www.rfi.fr/afrique-foot/20110219-chan-2011-niger-heroique-mais-elimine-le-soudan CHAN 2011 : Un Niger héroïque mais éliminé par le Soudan&nbsp;– Football/CHAN 2011&nbsp;– RFI] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120124005731/http://www.rfi.fr/afrique-foot/20110219-chan-2011-niger-heroique-mais-elimine-le-soudan |date=2012-01-24}} 2011-02-19</ref>

After home wins over [[South Africa national football team|South Africa]] and [[Sierra Leone national football team|Sierra Leone]], on 8 October 2011 Niger qualified for the Africa Cup of Nations for the first time in its history, despite losing 3–0 in Egypt.<ref>{{cite news |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/15229854.stm |title=Niger in historic qualification despite Cairo loss |work=BBC Sport |publisher=British Broadcasting Corporation |date=2011-10-08 |access-date=2011-10-08 |archive-date=2012-01-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120119215402/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/15229854.stm |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>[http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8613171.stm Fixtures, results and tables for the qualifiers for the 2012 Africa Cup of Nations to be co-hosted by Gabon and Equatorial Guinea.] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120128022128/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8613171.stm |date=2012-01-28}} BBC Sport.</ref> Niger, South Africa and Sierra Leone all ended with nine points, but Niger qualified thanks to their superior head-to-head record against their rivals.

At the [[2012 African Cup of Nations]], Niger was placed in Group C alongside co-hosts [[Gabon national football team|Gabon]], [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]] and [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]]. In their opening match, Niger lost 2–0 to Gabon, while against Tunisia in [[Libreville]], Niger trailed 1–0 on an early goal from [[Youssef Msakni]] in which he dribbled his way through for a fine goal after just four minutes. [[William N'Gounou]], however, then made history by scoring Niger's first ever goal at the African Cup of Nations. A 1–1 draw looked likely, but [[Issam Jemâa]]'s goal would eliminate Niger from the tournament. In the final match, Niger faced Morocco in a match featuring two sides already eliminated from the tournament. [[Younès Belhanda]] scored on an assist from [[Marouane Chamakh]] just 11 minutes from time to give Morocco a 1–0 victory.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/16803984|title=Niger 0-1 Morocco|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2021-11-15|archive-date=2021-11-15|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211115201343/https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/16803984|url-status=live}}</ref>

Later in 2012, Niger repeated its success in African Nations Cup qualifiers by beating [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]] in a two-legged series to qualify for the [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations]]. Guinea won the first match 1–0, but Niger won 2–0 in the second leg. Goalscorers [[Mohamed Chikoto]] and [[Issoufou Boubacar Garba|Issoufou Boubacar]] had sent Niger to another African Cup of Nations tournament.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.mtnfootball.com/africa/african-tournaments/2013-afcon-qualifiers/ |title=2013 Africa Cup of Nations Qualifiers - Qualifier Rounds - MTNFootball |access-date=2012-10-16 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121009102658/http://www.mtnfootball.com/africa/african-tournaments/2013-afcon-qualifiers/ |archive-date=2012-10-09 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

In their first match at the 2013 Africa Cup of Nations, Niger lost 1–0 to [[Mali national football team|Mali]] at the [[Nelson Mandela Bay Stadium]] in [[Port Elizabeth]]. Mali captain [[Seydou Keita (footballer)|Seydou Keita]] handed his nation the hard-fought victory five minutes before the end of the encounter. Niger then earned their first point ever at the African Cup of nations after holding [[DR Congo national football team|DR Congo]] to a 0–0 draw. In the third match, [[Ghana national football team|Ghana]] outclassed Niger 3–0 to reach the quarter-finals as Group B winners.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ghanaweb.com/GhanaHomePage/SportsArchive/artikel.php?ID=263428|title=Ghana cruises into African Cup quarters|date=January 28, 2013|website=GhanaWeb|access-date=November 15, 2021|archive-date=November 15, 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211115201337/https://www.ghanaweb.com/GhanaHomePage/SportsArchive/artikel.php?ID=263428|url-status=live}}</ref> Niger finished bottom of the group.

On 22 May 2014, Niger played a friendly match against [[Ukraine national football team|Ukraine]], marking the first ever match against a European nation. Oumarou Bale scored in the 56th minute, cancelling out a 20th-minute goal from [[Ivan Ordets]] before Ukraine won on a goal from [[Taras Stepanenko]] as the match finished 2–1.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://shakhtar.com/404.html?item=%2fnews%2f31806&user=extranet%5cAnonymous&site=fcsd|title=Страница не найдена|website=shakhtar.com|access-date=2021-11-15|archive-date=2021-11-15|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211115201339/https://shakhtar.com/404.html?item=/news/31806&user=extranet%5CAnonymous&site=fcsd|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2014301--ukraine-vs-niger/|title=Ukraine-Niger|access-date=2014-06-04|archive-date=2014-06-06|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606230835/http://www.uefa.com/friendlies/season=2014/matches/round=2000374/match=2014301/index.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Niger national football team results (2020–present)}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Football box collapsible
| date       = 5 September
| time       = {{UTZ|20:00|1}}
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| team1      = {{fb-rt|MAR}} 
| team2      = {{fb|NIG}} 
| score      = 5–0
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020793
| goals1     =
*[[Ismael Saibari|Saibari]] {{goal|29||38}}
*[[Ayoub El Kaabi|El Kaabi]] {{goal|51}}
*[[Hamza Igamane|Igamane]] {{goal|69}}
*[[Azzedine Ounahi|Ounahi]] {{goal|84}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[Prince Moulay Abdellah Stadium]]
| location   = [[Rabat]], Morocco
| attendance = 63,000
| referee    = Jean-Jacques Ndala Ngambo ([[Congolese Association Football Federation|DR Congo]])
| result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
| date       = 9 September
| time       = {{UTZ|16:00|3}}
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| team1      = {{fb-rt|TAN}} 
| team2      = {{fb|NIG}} 
| score      = 0–1
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020791
| goals1     =
| goals2     =
*[[Daniel Sosah|Sosah]] {{goal|58}}
| stadium    = [[Amaan Stadium]]
| location   = [[Zanzibar City]], Tanzania
| attendance = 
| referee    = [[Mustapha Ghorbal]] ([[Algerian Football Federation|Algeria]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
| date       = 8 October
| time       = {{UTZ|19:00|0}}
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| team1      = {{fb-rt|NIG}} 
| team2      = {{fb|CGO}} 
| score      = 3–1
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021055
| goals1     =
*[[Daniel Sosah|Sosah]] {{goal|45+2}}
*[[Youssouf Oumarou|Oumarou]] {{goal|49}}
*[[Victorien Adebayor|Adebayor]] {{goal|67}}
| goals2     =
*[[Déo Bassinga|Bassinga]] {{goal|90+4}}
| stadium    = [[Stade du 4 Août]]
| location   = [[Ouagadougou]], Burkina Faso
| attendance = 
| referee    = Boubou Traoré ([[Malian Football Federation|Mali]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
| date       = 12 October
| time       = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| team1      = {{fb-rt|ZAM}} 
| team2      = {{fb|NIG}} 
| score      = 0–1
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021054
| goals1     =
| goals2     =
*[[Daniel Sosah|Sosah]] {{goal|56}}
| stadium    = [[Levy Mwanawasa Stadium]]
| location   = [[Ndola]], Zambia
| attendance = 49,800
| referee    = Lucky Kasalirwe ([[Federation of Uganda Football Associations|Uganda]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 14 November
| time       = 
| team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
| score      = 3–2
| report     = https://www.espn.com/soccer/match/_/gameId/760878/niger-burkina-faso
| team2      = {{fb|NIG}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = 
| stadium    = [[El Bechir Stadium]]
| location   = [[Mohammedia]], Morocco
| attendance = 
| referee    = 
| result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 18 November
| time       = {{UTZ|16:30|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|GUI}}
| score      = 1–1
| team2      = {{fb|NIG}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Aliou Baldé|Baldé]] {{goal|59}}
| goals2     = 
*[[Oumar Sako|Sako]] {{goal|84}}
| stadium    = 
| location   = [[Berrechid]], Morocco
| attendance = [[Berrechid Municipal Stadium]]
| referee    = 
| report     = https://guineenews.org/2025/11/18/journee-fifa-la-guinee-tenue-en-echec-par-le-niger/
| result     = D
}}

===2026===
{{Football box collapsible
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date = 27 March
|time = {{UTZ|19:00|1}} 
|team1 = {{fb-rt|LBY}}
|score = 0–0
|team2 = {{fb|NIG}}
|goals1 = 
|goals2 =
|stadium = [[Larbi Zaouli Stadium]]
|location = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
|attendance =
|referee = Mustapha Kech Chaf ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]])
|report = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-27/libya-vs-niger/4129718
|result = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date = 31 March
|time = {{UTZ|17:00|1}} 
|team1 = {{fb-rt|NIG}}
|score = 0–1
|team2 = {{fb|TOG}}
|goals1 = 
|goals2 =
*[[Kevin Denkey|Denkey]] {{goal|5}}
|stadium = [[Père Jégo Stadium]]
|location = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
|attendance =
|referee = [[Mohamed El Baroudi]] ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]]) 
|report = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-31/niger-vs-togo/4129717
|result = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date = 5 June
|time = {{UTZ|20:00|1}} 
|team1 = {{fb-rt|BEN}}
|score = 1–1
|team2 = {{fb|NIG}}
|goals1 = 
*[[Amiro Amadou|Amadou]] {{goal|37}}
|goals2 =
*[[Oumar Sako|Sako]] {{goal|90+2}}
|stadium = [[Père Jégo Stadium]]
|location = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
|attendance =
|referee = Naoufal Nachite ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]]) 
|report = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-06-05/benin-vs-niger/4314449
|result = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date = 8 June
|time = {{UTZ|20:00|1}} 
|team1 = {{fb-rt|MRT}}
|score = 1–0
|team2 = {{fb|NIG}}
|goals1 = 
*[[Idrissa Thiam|I. Thiam]] {{goal|90+6}}
|goals2 =
|stadium = [[Père Jégo Stadium]]
|location = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
|attendance =
|referee = Mustapha Kechchaf ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]]) 
|report = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-06-08/mauritania-vs-niger/4310872
|result = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|NIG}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|MAR}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Niger
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MAR}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|NIG}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Morocco
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GAB}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|NIG}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Gabon
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|NIG}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|GAB}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Niger
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|NIG}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|LES}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Niger
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|LES}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|NIG}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Lesotho
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}

==Coaching history==

[[File:Rolland Courbis 001.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Rolland Courbis]] became the manager of the national football team of Niger in 2012]]

{{div col|colwidth=25em}}
* {{flagicon|Germany}} [[Heinz-Peter Uberjahn]] (1981–1986)
* {{flagicon|Niger}} [[Soumaila Tiemogo]] (1992)
* {{flagicon|Niger}} [[David Nadjadoum]] (1992–1994)
* {{flagicon|Niger}} [[Soumaila Tiemogo]] (1998)
* {{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Amadou Toure]] (1998)
* {{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Patrice Neveu]] (1999–2000)
* {{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Jean-Yves Chay]] (2000)
* {{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[Yeo Martial]] (2002–2003)<ref>{{cite news |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/2514163.stm |work=BBC News |title=Martial named Niger coach | date=26 November 2002 |access-date=17 October 2012 |archive-date=12 May 2021 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20210512044804/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/2514163.stm |url-status=live}}</ref>
* {{flagicon|Togo}} [[Bana Tchanile]] (2006–2007)
* {{flagicon|Niger}} [[Hamey Amadou]] (2007–2008)
* {{flagicon|Romania}} [[Dan Anghelescu]] (2008)
* {{flagicon|Niger}} Frederic Costa (2008–2009)
* {{flagicon|Niger}} [[Harouna Doula Gabde]] (2009–2012)
* {{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Rolland Courbis]] (2012)
* {{flagicon|Germany}} [[Gernot Rohr]] (2012–2014)
* {{flagicon|Niger}} [[Cheick Omar Diabate]] (2014–2015)
* {{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[François Zahoui]] (2015–2019)
* {{flagicon|France}} [[Jean-Guy Wallemme]] (2019–2020)
* {{flagicon|France}} [[Jean-Michel Cavalli]] (2020–2023)
* {{flagicon|Niger}} [[Zakaria Yaou Ibrahim]] (''Interim'') (2023)
* {{flagicon|Morocco}} [[Ezzaki Badou]] (2023–present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E]] match against [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]] and the [[Exhibition game#International football|friendly]] against [[Bonaire national football team|Bonaire]] on 21 and 25 March 2025.<ref>{{cite web |title=Publication officielle de la liste des joueurs convoqués #MENA pour la double confrontation contre le MAROC ET BONAIRE |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=1060278622807580&set=a.457879976380784 |website=Facebook |publisher=Fédération Nigérienne De Football |access-date=26 March 2025 |date=13 March 2025}}</ref>

''Caps and goals are correct as of 21 March 2025, after the match against [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Mahamadou Tanja]]|age={{bda|1996|7|5|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=0|club=[[AS FAN]]|clubnat=NIG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Yahaya Babari]]|age={{bda|1993|9|22|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[AS GNN]]|clubnat=NIG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abdoulaye Boureima Katkoré]]|age={{bda|1993|3|26|df=y}}|caps=53|goals=0|club=[[Al-Entesar Club]]|clubnat=KSA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abdoul Garba]]|age={{bda|2000|7|18|df=y}}|caps=34|goals=1|club=[[AS GNN]]|clubnat=NIG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Rahim Alhassane]]|age={{bda|2002|1|1|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=0|club=[[Real Oviedo|Oviedo]]|clubnat=ESP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[David Lebne]]|age={{birth date and age|1989|04|15|df=yes}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[AS FAN]]|clubnat=NIG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Oumar Sako]]|age={{bda|1996|5|4|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=2|club=[[FC Rostov|Rostov]]|clubnat=RUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abraham Adamou Gora]]|age={{bda|2002|9|14|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[US GN]]|clubnat=NIG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Mohamed Abdourahmane]]|age={{bda|2002|3|11|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Free agent]]}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Aboubacar Camara (footballer, born 2002)|Aboubacar Camara]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|01|04|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[KF Bylis|Bylis]]|clubnat=ALB}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abdoul Jalil Mamane]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|04|17|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AS Douanes (Niger)|Douanes]]|clubnat=NIG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Youssouf Oumarou]]|age={{bda|1993|2|16|df=y}}|caps=54|goals=5|club=[[Al-Karma SC|Al-Karma]]|clubnat=IRQ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ali Mohamed (footballer)|Ali Mohamed]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|10|7|df=y}}|caps=45|goals=0|club=[[Maccabi Haifa F.C.|Maccabi Haifa]]|clubnat=ISR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Abdoul Madjid Moumouni]]|age={{bda|1994|5|10|df=y}}|caps=28|goals=0|club=[[Al-Shorta SC|Al-Shorta]]|clubnat=IRQ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ousseini Badamassi]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|4|21|df=y}}|caps=15|goals=3|club=[[TP Mazembe|Mazembe]]|clubnat=COD}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Hassane Harouna Moussa]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|01|01|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[AS FAN]]|clubnat=NIG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Abdoul Latif Djibril]]|age={{bda|2005|7|23|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Free agent]]}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Zakariyaou Ibrahim]]|age={{bda|2005|7|30|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Gazelle FC (Cameroon)|Gazelle]]|clubnat=CMR}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Daniel Sosah]]|age={{bda|1998|9|21|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=8|club=[[FC Aktobe|Aktobe]]|clubnat=KAZ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Issa Djibrilla]]|age={{bda|1996|1|1|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=4|club=[[Zira FK|Zira]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Kairou Amoustapha]]|age={{bda|2001|1|1|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Hamrun Spartans F.C.|Hamrun Spartans]]|clubnat=MLT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Chamsoudine Loukoumane]]|age={{bda|1998|1|10|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Free agent]]}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for Niger in the last 12 months.
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Naim Van Attenhoven]]|age={{bda|2003|1|31|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Crossing Schaerbeek]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{fb|GHA}}, 18 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Younoussa Abiboulaye]]|age={{bda|2004|9|3|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[US GN]]|clubnat=NIG|latest=v. {{fb|ANG}}, 15 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Abdoul Kahar Issoufou]]|age={{bda|1999|9|7|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[AS Police de Bamako|AS Police]]|clubnat=NIG|latest=v. {{fb|ANG}}, 15 October 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Moumouni Darankoum]]|age={{bda|2002|8|7|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=0|club=[[Mesaimeer SC|Al-Mesaimeer]]|clubnat=QAT|latest=v. {{fb|GHA}}, 18 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Najeeb Yakubu]]|age={{bda|2000|5|1|df=y}}|caps=15|goals=0|club=[[Persijap Jepara|Persijap]]|clubnat=IDN|latest=v. {{fb|GHA}}, 18 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Alhabib Hassane Abdou]]|age={{bda|2003|8|24|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Al Ittihad Alexandria Club|Al Ittihad]]|clubnat=EGY|latest=v. {{fb|GHA}}, 18 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Amadou Salissou]]|age={{bda|2003|11|28|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Free agent]]|latest=v. {{fb|GHA}}, 18 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abdel Rahim Bonkano]]|age={{bda|2002|1|1|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=0|club=[[Real Oviedo|Oviedo]]|clubnat=ESP|latest=v. {{fb|ANG}}, 15 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abdoulaye Karim Doudou]]|age={{bda|1998|9|25|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[Al-Minaa SC|Al-Minaa]]|clubnat=IRQ|latest=v. {{fb|ANG}}, 15 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Philippe Boueye]]|age={{bda|2003|9|23|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[FC Prishtina|Prishtina]]|clubnat=KOS|latest=v. {{fb|ANG}}, 15 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abdoul Kader Kassali]]|age={{bda|1998|9|30|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[AS GNN|GNN]]|clubnat=NIG|latest=v. {{fb|ANG}}, 15 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Inoussa Amadou]]|age={{bda|2000|9|5|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AS FAN]]|clubnat=NIG|latest=v. {{fb|ANG}}, 15 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Boubacar Hama Hainikoye]]|age={{bda|2002|5|17|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[US GN]]|clubnat=NIG|latest=v. {{fb|GHA}}, 9 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Mohamed Idrissa Karimou]]|age={{bda|1993|4|6|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[US GN]]|clubnat=NIG|latest=v. {{fb|CGO}}, 6 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Yussif Moussa]]|age={{bda|1998|9|4|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=1|club=[[Al-Talaba SC|Al-Talaba]]|clubnat=IRQ|latest=v. {{fb|GHA}}, 18 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Salim Abubakar]]|age={{bda|2003|4|6|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[KF Skënderbeu Korçë|Skënderbeu Korçë]]|clubnat=ALB|latest=v. {{fb|GHA}}, 18 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Moussa Kassa Moudou]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|11|11|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[US GN]]|clubnat=NIG|latest=v. {{fb|GHA}}, 18 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Maarouf Salissou]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|11|5|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[AS FAN]]|clubnat=NIG|latest=v. {{fb|GHA}}, 18 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Salifou Danja]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|1|31|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[US GN]]|clubnat=NIG|latest=v. {{fb|GHA}}, 18 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ousmane Diabaté (footballer, born 1994)|Ousmane Diabaté]]|age={{bda|1994|7|9|df=y}}|caps=44|goals=0|club=[[Al-Sadaqa Shahat FC|Al-Sadaqa]]|clubnat=LBY|latest=v. {{fb|ANG}}, 15 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Abdel Nasser Chaibou]]|age={{bda|2000|1|3|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[US GN]]|clubnat=NIG|latest=v. {{fb|ANG}}, 15 October 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Boubacar Haïnikoye]]|age={{bda|1998|10|7|df=y}}|caps=32|goals=3|club=[[TP Mazembe]]|clubnat=DRC|latest=v. {{fb|GHA}}, 18 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Zakari Junior Lambo]]|age={{bda|1999|1|19|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=1|club=[[KSC Lokeren (2025)|Lokeren-Temse]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{fb|ANG}}, 15 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Victorien Adebayor]]|age={{bda|1996|11|12|df=y}}|caps=55|goals=20|club=[[Singida Black Stars FC|Singida Black Stars]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=v. {{fb|CGO}}, 6 June 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Boubacar Djibril Goumey]]|age={{bda|2000|7|14|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=2|club=[[Zakho SC|Zakho]]|clubnat=IRQ|latest=v. {{fb|CGO}}, 6 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
<sup>DEC</sup> Player refused to join the team after the call-up.<br/>
<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.<br/>
<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.<br/>
<sup>RET</sup> Player has retired from international football.<br/>
<sup>SUS</sup> Suspended from the national team.
{{nat fs end|background=}}

==Records==
{{Updated|31 March 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Niger |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/134/Niger.html |website=National Football Teams |access-date=2022-06-15 |archive-date=2023-09-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230909105809/https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/134/2023/Niger.html |url-status=live}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Niger.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:170px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| [[Kassaly Daouda]] || 89 || 0 || 2002–2022
|-
| 2 ||  align=left| '''[[Youssouf Oumarou]]''' || 75 || 8 || 2013–present
|-
| 3 ||align=left| '''[[Abdoulaye Boureima Katkoré|Abdoulaye Katkoré]]''' || 74 || 0 || 2014–present 
|-
| 4 ||align=left| [[Koffi Dan Kowa]] || 67 || 4 || 2008–2018
|-
| 5 || align=left| '''[[Victorien Adebayor]]''' || 64 || 22 || 2015–present
|-
| 6 ||align=left| '''[[Amadou Moutari]]''' || 60 || 3 || 2012–present
|-
| 7 ||align=left| [[Moussa Maâzou]] || 54 || 13 || 2008–2021
|-
| rowspan=2|8 ||align=left| [[Karim Lancina|Lassina Konaté]] || 53 || 1 || 2006–2016
|-
|align=left| [[Souleymane Dela Sacko|Souleymane Sacko]] || 53 || 3 || 2007–2019
|-
| 10 || align=left| '''[[Ali Mohamed (footballer)|Ali Mohamed]]''' || 52 || 0 || 2013–present
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:170px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| '''[[Victorien Adebayor]]''' || 22 || 64 || {{#expr:22/64 round 2}} || 2015–present
|-
| 2 ||align=left| '''[[Daniel Sosah]]''' || 15 || 34 || {{#expr:15/34 round 2}} || 2021–present
|-
| 3 ||align=left| [[Moussa Maâzou]] || 13 || 54 || {{#expr:13/54 round 2}} || 2008–2021
|-
| 4 ||align=left| [[Kamilou Daouda]] || 10 || 37 || {{#expr:10/37 round 2}} || 2007–2019
|-
| 5 ||align=left| '''[[Youssouf Oumarou]]''' || 8 || 75 || {{#expr:8/75 round 2}} || 2013–present
|-
| 6 ||align=left| [[Mounkaila Ide Barkire]] || 7 || 13 || {{#expr:7/13 round 2}} || 1992–1998
|-
| rowspan=3|7 ||align=left| [[Zakari Lambo]] || 6 || 10 || {{#expr:6/10 round 2}}|| 1990–1995
|-
|align=left| '''[[Issa Djibrilla]]''' || 6 || 32 || {{#expr:6/32 round 2}} || 2020–present
|-
| align=left| [[Mohamed Wonkoye]] || 6 || 49 || {{#expr:6/49 round 2}} || 2012–present 
|-
| 10 ||align=left| [[Mahamane Cissé]] || 5 || 38 || {{#expr:5/38 round 2}} || 2013–2022
|-
|}

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{{col-begin}}
{{col-3}}
{{col-end}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=41|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]]
|colspan=8|''Part of {{fb|France}}''||colspan=6|''Part of {{fb|France}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Chile}} [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]
|colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|[[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]] to [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Argentina}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|2
|1
|0
|1
|3
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
|6
|2
|2
|2
|4
|7
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy|1946}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|4
|2
|1
|1
|3
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|rowspan=6 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|7
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|6
|1
|0
|5
|5
|11
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|6
|1
|1
|4
|6
|12
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|4
|2
|1
|1
|6
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|6||2||1||3||13||17
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|8||5||0||3||11||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!
!{{Tooltip|0/15|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!
!
!
!
!
!
!44
!16
!6
!22
!51
!75
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{{main|Niger at the Africa Cup of Nations}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
!colspan=9| [[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{Flagicon|Zaire}} 1955
|
|
|
|
|
|
|
|
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 African Cup of Nations|1957]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Part of {{fb|France}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|United Arab Republic}} [[1959 African Cup of Nations|1959]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1962 Africa Cup of Nations|1962]]||rowspan=3 colspan=8|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1965 Africa Cup of Nations|1965]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1968 African Cup of Nations|1968]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1970 African Cup of Nations|1970]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon|1961}} [[1972 African Cup of Nations|1972]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt|1972}} [[1974 African Cup of Nations|1974]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 African Cup of Nations|1976]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 African Cup of Nations|1978]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 African Cup of Nations|1980]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Libya|1977}} [[1982 African Cup of Nations|1982]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[1984 African Cup of Nations|1984]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[1986 African Cup of Nations|1986]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 African Cup of Nations|1988]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 African Cup of Nations|1990]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 African Cup of Nations|1992]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 African Cup of Nations|1994]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 African Cup of Nations|1996]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|-
|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 African Cup of Nations|1998]]
|colspan=8|''Disqualified for withdrawing in 1996''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 African Cup of Nations|2000]] to <br> {{flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]
|rowspan=2| Group stage || 15th || 3 || 0 || 0 || 3 || 1 || 5
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|| 15th || 3 || 0 || 1 || 2 || 0 || 4
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 African Cup of Nations|2015]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=6|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
|-
|{{Flagicon|MAR}}{{flagicon|ALG}} {{Flagicon|TUN}} [[2029 Africa Cup of Nations|2029]]
|-
!Total||Group stage||2/35||6||0||1||5||1||9
|}

[[File:Morocco vs Niger, February 09 2011-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Niger national team against [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]], 9 February 2011]]

===African Nations Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[African Nations Championship]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 4
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2009 African Nations Championship|2009]]||colspan="8"|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan}} [[2011 African Nations Championship|2011]]||Quarter-finals||7th||4||2||1||1||3||3
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]]||colspan="8"|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda}} [[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]]||Group stage||16th ||3||0||1||2||3||11
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2018 African Nations Championship|2018]]||colspan="8"|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]]||Group stage||12th||3||0||2||1||2||3
|-  style="background:#9acdff"
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]]||'''Fourth place'''||'''4th'''||'''5'''||'''2'''||'''1'''||'''2'''||'''3'''||'''6'''
|-
!'''Total'''
!'''Fourth place'''
!'''4/7'''
!'''15'''
!'''4'''
!'''5'''
!'''6'''
!'''11'''
!'''23'''
|}

===UEMOA Tournament===
{{Main|UEMOA Tournament}}

==Head-to-head record==
{{updated|5 June 2026}}
{|class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center"
!Opponent
!Games
!Wins
!Draws
!Losses
!Goals For
!Goals Against
!Goal Differential
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ALG}}
|10
|1
|1
|8
|3
|27
| −24
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ANG}}
|4
|0
|0
|4
|2
|8
| −6
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BEN}}
|16
|3
|6
|7
|18
|25
| −7
|- bgcolor=#CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BON}}
|1
|1
|0
|0
|6
|0
| +6
|- bgcolor=#CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BOT}}
|3
|2
|1
|0
|4
|2
| +2
|- bgcolor=#CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BDI}}
|1
|1
|0
|0
|3
|1
| +2
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BFA}}
|19
|3
|8
|9
|17
|29
| −12
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|CMR}}
|3
|0
|1
|2
|0
|4
| −4
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|CTA}}
|3
|0
|2
|1
|3
|5
| −2
|- bgcolor=#CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|CHA}}
|2
|1
|1
|0
|2
|1
| +1
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|CGO}}
|7
|1
|2
|4
|7
|13
| −6
|- bgcolor=#CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|DJI}}
|2
|2
|0
|0
|11
|4
| +7
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|DR Congo}}
|3
|1
|1
|1
|4
|2
| +2
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|EGY}}
|7
|1
|1
|5
|2
|19
| −17
|- bgcolor=#FFFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|EQG}}
|1
|0
|1
|0
|1
|1
| 0
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ETH}}
|5
|2
|0
|3
|4
|7
| −3
|- bgcolor=#CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SWZ}}
|2
|1
|1
|0
|2
|1
| +1
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|GAB}}
|8
|2
|0
|6
|10
|19
| −9
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|GAM}}
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|3
| -2
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|GHA}}
|13
|1
|2
|10
|7
|40
| −33
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|GUI}}
|8
|3
|0
|6
|9
|14
| −5
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|CIV}}
|13
|0
|2
|11
|9
|29
| −20
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|LES}}
|2
|1
|0
|1
|3
|3
| 0
|- bgcolor=#FFFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|Liberia}}
|8
|3
|2
|3
|7
|10
| −3
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|Libya}}
|8
|0
|5
|3
|7
|13
| −6
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|Madagascar}}
|2
|0
|1
|1
|2
|6
| −4
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|Mali}}
|8
|1
|2
|5
|4
|10
| −6
|- bgcolor=#CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|Mauritania}}
|5
|3
|2
|0
|10
|3
| +7
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|Morocco}}
|9
|1
|0
|8
|3
|23
| −20
|- bgcolor=#FFFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|MOZ}}
|1
|0
|1
|0
|1
|1
| 0
|- bgcolor=#CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|NAM}}
|1
|1
|0
|0
|2
|1
| +1
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|Nigeria}}
|3
|0
|0
|3
|1
|7
| −6
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SEN}}
|7
|1
|2
|4
|3
|7
| −4
|- bgcolor=#CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|Sierra Leone}}
|7
|4
|0
|3
|12
|15
| −3
|- bgcolor=#CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SOM}}
|3
|1
|2
|0
|4
|1
| +1
|- bgcolor=#FFFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|South Africa}}
|3
|1
|1
|1
|2
|3
| −1
|- bgcolor=#FFFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SUD}}
|2
|1
|0
|1
|4
|1
| +3
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|TAN}}
|4
|1
|1
|2
|2
|3
| -1
|- bgcolor=#CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|Togo}}
|12
|4
|3
|5
|17
|18
| -1
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|TUN}}
|3
|0
|0
|3
|2
|4
| −2
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|UGA}}
|8
|2
|2
|4
|7
|11
| -4
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|UKR}}
|1
|0
|0
|1
|1
|2
| −1
|- bgcolor=#FFCCCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|UAE}}
|1
|0
|0
|1
|0
|4
| −4
|- bgcolor=#CCFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ZAM}}
|2
|2
|0
|0
|3
|1
| +2
|- bgcolor=#FFFFCC
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ZIM}}
|1
|0
|1
|0
|1
|1
| +0
|-class="sortbottom"
! 45 Countries || 235 || 53 || 54 || 127 || 224 || 401 || −177
|}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[West African Nations Cup]]'''
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (1): 1986
* '''[[UEMOA Tournament]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (1)''': 2010
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (2): 2007, 2009

==Notes==
{{notelist}}

==References==
{{reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons category|Niger national association football team|Niger national football team}}
* [https://fenifoot.football/index.html Niger Football Federation website] – official website
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/nig/ Niger] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/NIG Niger] at [[FIFA]]

{{Niger national football team}}
{{Niger squad 2012 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Niger squad 2013 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Football in Niger}}
{{2013 Africa Cup of Nations finalists}}
{{WAFU Football}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Niger}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Niger National Football Team}}
[[Category:Niger national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
